assertion_results.text: Version impact table shows mixed results: 2.1.x versions marked YES (affected) and 2.2.x versions marked NO (not affected) based on lock file evidence
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md shows a table with 2.1.0 and 2.1.1 marked 'YES' (h2 0.4.5 < 0.4.8) and all 2.2.x versions (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) marked 'NO' (h2 >= 0.4.8). Summary confirms 'Stream 2.1.x: All versions are AFFECTED' and 'Stream 2.2.x: All versions are NOT AFFECTED'. Lock file evidence is cited: 'extracted via git show <tag>:Cargo.lock | grep -A2 name = "h2"'.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction includes only the actually affected versions (2.1.x versions), not the unaffected 2.2.x versions (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md shows 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.1.0, RHTPA 2.1.1]' — only 2.1.x versions. The diff table explicitly shows RHTPA 2.2.0 as 'Removed' with reason 'h2 0.4.8, NOT affected (>= 0.4.8)' and RHTPA 2.1.1 as 'Added' with reason 'h2 0.4.5, affected (< 0.4.8)'. No 2.2.x versions remain in the proposed set.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks are created only for the affected stream (2.1.x), not for 2.2.x which already ships the patched version (Step 8 Case A scoping)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md states 'Case A: Affected -- create remediation tasks for stream 2.1.x only.' Task 1 targets 'release/0.3.z' (2.1.x upstream branch) and Task 2 targets 'rhtpa-release.0.3.z' (2.1.x release repo). A dedicated section 'No Remediation for 2.2.x' explicitly confirms: 'Stream 2.2.x does not receive remediation tasks because the version impact table shows all 2.2.x versions ship h2 >= 0.4.8.'

assertion_results.text: The analysis treats the issue as unscoped (no stream suffix) and checks ALL versions across all streams (Step 1 Stream scope resolution, unscoped path)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md states: 'The issue summary contains no stream suffix... this issue is treated as unscoped -- it covers all configured version streams' and lists both Stream 2.1.x and Stream 2.2.x. version-impact.md confirms: 'Since the issue is unscoped (no stream suffix), ALL versions across ALL streams are analyzed' and the table includes all 7 versions: 2.1.0, 2.1.1 (stream 2.1.x) and 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 (stream 2.2.x).

assertion_results.text: Cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated because the issue is unscoped — it covers all streams by definition (Step 8 Case B applies only to scoped issues)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md contains an explicit section 'Cross-stream impact notice' stating: 'A cross-stream impact notice is NOT generated for this issue because it is unscoped -- an unscoped issue covers all streams by definition. Cross-stream impact notices (Step 8 Case B) apply only to scoped issues where the triage reveals impact outside the issue's declared stream scope. Since TC-8004 has no stream suffix, there is no "outside scope" to report.'
